name: vscode-window-flash-notify
description: Flash the current VS Code window from terminal, SSH, VM, or background scripts without stealing focus.

commands.title: Window Flash Notify: Copy Curl Command

windowFlashNotify.basePort.description: First port to try when starting the local notification HTTP server.

windowFlashNotify.portSearchRange.description: Number of ports to try, starting from windowFlashNotify.basePort.

windowFlashNotify.listenHost.description: Host address for the local HTTP server. Keep 127.0.0.1 unless your VM cannot reach the host loopback via its gateway.

windowFlashNotify.gatewayHost.description: Host address that guests such as Vagrant/VirtualBox VMs can use to reach this machine.

windowFlashNotify.authToken.description: Optional token required in the X-Window-Flash-Token header or token query parameter.

windowFlashNotify.flashUntilForeground.description: When enabled, flash the taskbar until the VS Code window becomes foreground.

windowFlashNotify.flashCount.description: Number of flashes to request when windowFlashNotify.flashUntilForeground is disabled.

windowFlashNotify.showInternalNotificati.description: Also show a VS Code internal notification after receiving a request.

windowFlashNotify.showToast.description: Also show a native desktop notification. Clicking the notification attempts to focus the matching VS Code window.

windowFlashNotify.toastTimeout.description: Native desktop notification timeout in seconds.

windowFlashNotify.writePortFile.description: Write .vscode/window-flash-notify-port.json into each workspace folder for scripts to discover the server.
